senses.gloss: to be truthful, to speak the truth

examples.text: قَالَ سَنَنْظُرُ أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
examples.english: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.

examples.text: صَدِيقُكَ مَنْ صَدَقَكَ لَا مَنْ صَدَّقَكَ
examples.english: Your friend is he who is trustworthy, not he who trusts you.
He told me the truth in his speech.

senses.gloss: to believe, to accept that (someone) is telling the truth
to believe, to accept as true

examples.text: هل تقدر أن تصدِّق الكتاب المقدس؟

إِنَّ سَعْيَكُمْ لَشَتَّى
 فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَى وَٱتَّقَى
 وَصَدَّقَ بِٱلْحُسْنَى
 فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَى
 وَأَمَّا مَن بَخِلَ وَٱسْتَغْنَى
 وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلْحُسْنَى
 فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْعُسْرَى
examples.english:
Lo! your effort is dispersed (toward divers ends).
As for him who giveth and is dutiful (toward Allah)
And believeth in goodness;
Surely We will ease his way unto the state of ease.
But as for him who hoardeth and deemeth himself independent,
And disbelieveth in goodness;
Surely We will ease his way unto adversity.
